name: Irta Trail (PR-CV 194)
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra d'Irta — Circular por el Sendero de Irta (PR-CV 194)
description: Hike the difficult 14.1-mile Irta Trail (PR-CV 194) in Serra d'Irta Natural Park, featuring coastal views, pine forests, and historic towers
region_intro: Hiking trails around Peníscola / Peñíscola traverse a unique landscape where the Mediterranean Sea meets mountains. The region is characterized by the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, offering unspoiled coastline with dramatic cliffs, clear coves, and Mediterranean vegetation. Inland, the Marjal de Peñíscola provides tranquil wetlands for easy walks. This blend of coastal and mountainous terrain offers diverse hiking opportunities. Best hiking trails around Peníscola / Peñíscola The most popular hiking route is Peñíscola Marshland Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 25 minutes to complete. This easy path combines views of the historic castle with the tranquil wetlands of La Marjal. Another top favourite among local hikers is Irta Trail (PR-CV 194), a difficult 14.1 miles (22.7 km) path. This route offers extensive coastal views, pine forests, and passes by historical towers within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park. Local hikers also love the View of Prebet Beach – Badum Tower loop from Urbanització Font Nova, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Peníscola / Peñíscola is defined by its blend of sea and mountains, the protected Sierra de Irta Natural Park, and coastal wetlands. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging mountain paths. The routes in Peníscola / Peñíscola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Peníscola / Peñíscola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: There are over 110 hiking trails in Peníscola / Peñíscola, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy coastal strolls to more challenging mountain paths within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: The best months for hiking in Peníscola / Peñíscola are typically March, April, and May. During spring, the weather is pleasant, and the natural landscapes are vibrant with blooms. Autumn also offers comfortable temperatures for exploring the trails.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Peníscola / Peñíscola offers many easy trails. For a gentle walk that combines historical views with tranquil wetlands, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e878721090/peniscola-marshland-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñíscola Marshland Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This 3.4-mile route is perfect for families and takes about 1 hour 25 minutes to complete. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10385280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñíscola Beach Promenade&lt;/a&gt; loop, which offers flat terrain and sea views.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking?
A: Peníscola / Peñíscola is unique for its blend of sea and mountains. You'll encounter the unspoiled coastline of the Sierra de Irta Natural Park with dramatic cliffs, hidden coves like Cala Puerto Negro and Cala Argilaga, and Mediterranean vegetation. Inland, the Marjal de Peñíscola offers tranquil wetlands, lagoons, and canals, ideal for birdwatching.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1672290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Prebet Beach – Badum Tower loop from Urbanització Font Nova&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 5.8-mile trail takes you through coastal landscapes and offers stunning views, typically completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I discover on the trails?
A: The hiking trails often lead to fascinating historical sites. Within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, you can find ancient watchtowers like the Badum Tower, which offers spectacular panoramic views. The park also contains historical remains such as Polpis Castle and Xivert Castle. Don't miss the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3014731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñíscola Castle&lt;/a&gt;, perched on a rock overlooking the sea, which is easily accessible from many routes.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: Many trails in Peníscola / Peñíscola, especially within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Please check specific trail regulations for any restrictions.

Q: What are the most challenging hikes in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e878641383/irta-trail-pr-cv-194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irta Trail (PR-CV 194)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.1-mile route. It traverses the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, offering extensive coastal views, pine forests, and passes by historical towers. This trail involves significant elevation changes and requires a good level of fitness.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I spot while hiking?
A: The Sierra de Irta Natural Park is rich in biodiversity, with unique flora and protected native fauna. In the Marjal de Peñíscola wetlands, you might spot various bird species, including protected ones like the grey heron and bittern. Keep an eye out for Mediterranean wildlife throughout the park's diverse ecosystems.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: The hiking routes in Peníscola / Peñíscola are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning coastal views, the diverse landscapes where mountains meet the sea, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various abilities.

Q: Are there any coastal trails with sea views?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Peníscola / Peñíscola offer spectacular sea views, particularly those within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38251153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí del Pebret Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, providing impressive vistas along the unspoiled coastline.
tips: One of the highest points within the Serra d'Irta Natural Park with simply amazing views. From here you can enjoy beautiful panoramic views of the coast and the park, one of the few undeveloped coastal places in the Valencian Community.
Beautiful viewpoint a little further south of Peñiscola. Located in the middle of the Parc Natural de la Serra d'Irta. You can hike there or ride a bike.

faqs.question: How long should I plan for hiking the Natural Park of Serra d'Irta — Circular via the Irta Trail (PR-CV 194)?
faqs.answer: This circular hike typically takes around 6 hours and 39 minutes to complete. However, given its challenging nature and the stunning views, you might want to allow extra time for breaks and enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on the Irta Trail (PR-CV 194)?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult. You'll encounter a diverse mix of terrain, including rugged limestone hills, coastal paths, and sections through pine forests and Mediterranean scrubland. Expect some steep inclines and rocky paths, especially as the route alternates between dramatic cliffs and secluded coves. It's a demanding but rewarding hike.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Irta Trail (PR-CV 194)?
faqs.answer: While the exact starting point for parking isn't specified, many routes within the Natural Park of Serra d'Irta, including sections of the PR-CV 194, are often accessed from areas around Alcossebre. Look for designated parking areas near the park entrances or trailheads in that vicinity.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Irta Trail (PR-CV 194)?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed in the Natural Park of Serra d'Irta. However, it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the rugged terrain and potential for encountering other hikers or protected species like Mediterranean tortoises.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Irta Trail (PR-CV 194)?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during the spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the temperatures are milder, making the long distance and elevation gain more manageable. Summer can be very hot, and some sections may offer little shade.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Natural Park of Serra d'Irta?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Natural Park of Serra d'Irta or to hike the Irta Trail (PR-CV 194). The park is freely accessible to the public, allowing everyone to enjoy its natural beauty and historical sites.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers breathtaking panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and the rugged coastline. Key highlights include the Alto de la Bota Summit, which provides extensive views, and the historic Badum Tower, a watchtower that once protected against pirate attacks. You'll also pass by secluded coves with crystal-clear waters.

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna can I expect to see in Serra d'Irta Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The park is home to diverse native flora and fauna. You might spot protected species such as Mediterranean tortoises, and the vegetation includes various species like devil's lupine. The unspoiled environment provides a rich habitat for many birds and other small animals.

faqs.question: Does the Irta Trail (PR-CV 194) intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route of the Natural Park of Serra d'Irta — Circular via the Irta Trail (PR-CV 194) overlaps with several other trails. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the Sender d'Irta (98%) and also passes through sections of El Camí IT6TR3 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat (51%), Senda de la Mola de Sant Antoni (14%), and Camí de Sant Antoni (14%).

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginner hikers?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant distance (over 22 km), and considerable elevation gain (over 500m), this specific circular route is generally not recommended for families with young children or beginner hikers. While some shorter, less challenging routes exist within Serra d'Irta, the PR-CV 194 requires a good level of fitness and hiking experience.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources along the Irta Trail (PR-CV 194)?
faqs.answer: The Natural Park of Serra d'Irta is known for its undeveloped and pristine coastline. As such, expect very limited facilities directly on the trail. It's crucial to carry all necessary water, snacks, and supplies with you. Plan to use facilities before or after your hike in nearby towns like Alcossebre.
